Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Indepently of my comment on function name, I am not sure it is the right way to solve the bug. May be we should avoid to register a new restore if we are in this case? i.e. avoid orphan restore handle.
Also normally if status is incompatible with a restore we should not hold a layout lock.

Patch Set 2:

Hello J-C, I am not sure to understand how you think we could better handle a valid sequence like "lfs hsm_restore <file>; lfs_hsm_cancel <file>; lfs hsm_restore <file>" ??
Also, we may think about to delay layout_lock at the time restore request will be identified as compatible and being sent to CT, but presently this occurs in CDT context and thus we do not expect to wait for layout_lock there, right?

Patch Set 2:

Instead of aquiring a second time layout lock :
- when receving a restore request
- search for a restore handle
- if one found, behave as for a duplicate request

BTW since, as you stated, mdt_agent_llog_update_rec() no longer exists and in-place action LLOG records update has been finally implemented, I should (now that it is not needed to return LLOG_DEL_RECORD for current record removal in llog_process_thread() routine) move my change back to the beginning of hsm_restore_cb() routine in order to not need taking layout_lock before record update and then to release it upon error!

Bruno, when CT received a cancel request it does it or it replies ENOTSUPP but it does not agree and do it later. it will be difficult to support sheater CT. Another solution is to do both, first check as I suggest, and also keep your proposition.

commit.subject: LU-8010 mdt: fix orphan layout_lock cases for restore
commit.message:
LU-8010 mdt: fix orphan layout_lock cases for restore

Previously to this patch layout was not unlocked when a restore
was failed before being sent to CT, leading to a situation where
other requestors hang and also to an orphan restore_handle to be
kept on CDT's list of registered restore actions.
Only way to clear situation then, was to stop CDT.
This situation could at least occur if a restore was canceled
but the CT does not handle cancel operation, allowing the restore
to complete but also to have new restore requests to be
registered in the mean time and then to be failed due to
incompatible (no longer released) file state.

Also fix similar deadlock cases where layout lock was taken for
previously started restore requests upon CDT restart, by forcing
their replay/restart. This should also strengthen overall HSM
recovery process.

Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ib1ba9156793a230d256ff80d74372813f10b0321
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/19710
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: jacques-Charles Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Reviewed-by: Aurelien Degremont <aurelien.degremont@cea.fr>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
